festivals.name: Bon Om Touk (Water Festival)
festivals.description: Cambodia's Water Festival reversal celebration draws 1 million people for the world's largest boat races in Phnom Penh.

festivals.name: Khmer New Year
festivals.description: Three days of water fights, traditional games, and offerings to the Buddha mark the Khmer New Year.

destinations.name: Angkor Wat & Siem Reap
destinations.description: The world's largest religious monument — the Khmer Empire's 12th-century temple complex is Cambodia's soul.

destinations.name: Phnom Penh
destinations.description: Cambodia's capital has the Royal Palace, the National Museum, and the sobering Tuol Sleng Genocide Museum.

destinations.name: Koh Rong
destinations.description: A jungle-covered island with white-sand beaches and bioluminescent plankton creating natural night-time light shows.

destinations.name: Kampot
destinations.description: A sleepy riverside town famous for the world's finest pepper, colonial architecture, and nearby Bokor National Park.

destinations.name: Battambang
destinations.description: Cambodia's most charming provincial city has colonial arcades, the world-famous bamboo train, and a thriving circus school.

destinations.name: Kep
destinations.description: A former French colonial seaside resort famous for its crab market and access to Koh Tonsay (Rabbit Island).

destinations.name: Mondulkiri
destinations.description: Rolling highlands with Bunong indigenous villages, ethical elephant sanctuaries, and stunning jungle waterfalls.

destinations.name: Kratie
destinations.description: A quiet Mekong town where you can kayak with endangered Irrawaddy dolphins in one of their last habitats.
